2606.30587.title: Words Speak Louder Than Code: Investigating Cognitive Heuristics in LLM-Based Code Vulnerability Detection
2606.30587.paper_summary: Researchers and practitioners increasingly apply Large Language Models (LLMs) for automated vulnerability detection. Recent work has shown that LLMs are susceptible to the same cognitive heuristics that bias human judgment. Yet, no work has investigated whether these heuristics affect a model's assessment of code vulnerabilities. In this paper, we present the first systematic exploration of cognitive heuristics in LLM-driven code vulnerability detection. We introduce a controlled framework that holds the code fixed and only varies the surrounding context to trigger three cognitive heuristics: the halo effect through author attribution, the framing effect through task objectives and consequences, and the anchoring effect through prior analysis results. Within this framework, we evaluate eight LLMs across three programming languages and perform both quantitative and code-level analyses. Our findings demonstrate that all evaluated models are susceptible to these heuristics. Cross-model average susceptibility is highest for framing at 33.2%, followed by anchoring at 23.5% and halo at 18.4%. Code-level analysis reveals that vulnerabilities that require semantic reasoning for detection are more susceptible to cognitive heuristics than those identifiable through pattern matching. Furthermore, models often change their verdict from safe to vulnerable based on the cognitive condition, without accurately identifying the actual vulnerability. To highlight the practical impact, we demonstrate a proof-of-concept black-box cognitive attack that can suppress up to 97% of previously detected vulnerabilities. These findings indicate that cognitive susceptibility is a consistent and exploitable property of LLM-based vulnerability detection.
2606.30587.paper_summary_zh: 研究人員和實務工作者越來越多地應用大型語言模型（LLMs）進行自動化漏洞檢測。最近的研究顯示，LLMs 容易受到與人類判斷偏見相同的認知啟發式影響。然而，尚未有研究調查這些啟發式是否影響模型對代碼漏洞的評估。在本文中，我們首次系統性地探索 LLM 驅動的代碼漏洞檢測中的認知啟發式。我們介紹了一個控制框架，固定代碼並僅變化周圍上下文，以觸發三種認知啟發式：通過作者歸因的光環效應、通過任務目標和後果的框架效應，以及通過先前分析結果的錨定效應。在這個框架內，我們評估了八個 LLM 在三種編程語言上的表現，並進行了定量和代碼層級的分析。我們的發現表明，所有評估的模型都容易受到這些啟發式的影響。跨模型的平均易感性對框架效應最高，達到 33.2%，其次是錨定效應 23.5% 和光環效應 18.4%。代碼層級分析顯示，需要語義推理來檢測的漏洞比通過模式匹配識別的漏洞更容易受到認知啟發式的影響。此外，模型經常根據認知條件將其判斷從安全變為脆弱，而未能準確識別實際漏洞。為了突出實際影響，我們展示了一個概念驗證的黑箱認知攻擊，該攻擊可以抑制多達 97% 先前檢測到的漏洞。這些發現表明，認知易感性是基於 LLM 的漏洞檢測的一個一致且可利用的特性。
2606.30587.author: Asif Shahriar et.al.
2606.30587.authors: Asif Shahriar, Hongyu Cai, Hadjer Benkraouda, Gang Wang, Z. Berkay Celik
